reasoning: The document contains multiple major defects that require human intervention. Both LLM and programmatic signals identified a bulleted list broken across a page break (pages 3 to 4). Additionally, programmatic detectors found that headers/footers were retained in the output and that two images have truncated, incomplete alt text. Because there are between 1 and 5 major defects, the document must be routed to HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.analysis: A bulleted list item spanning the page boundary between page 3 and page 4 is incorrectly split. The first half of the item is captured as a list item on page 3, but its continuation on page 4 is extracted as a standalone paragraph, completely breaking it out of the list structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Groundwater Assessments – The National Ash Management Advisory Board stated... existing conditions at these sites do not present a substantial likelihood that death...
concerns.output_quote:
Page 3 list item ends with: "...existing conditions at these sites do not present a"
Page 4 begins with a paragraph: "substantial likelihood that death, serious illness, severe personal injury, or a substantial endangerment to health, property, or the environment will occur."
concerns.location: Pages 3-4, Written Public Comment Summary list

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (4): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 4; Page 3 of 4
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=4, pages_affected=4/4. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p3→p4: list item ends “…nditions at these sites do not present a”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 2 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (2 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...g a large green 'N' and dark blue 'C' with a white"; p1: "...e green on the left to dark navy blue on the right"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=2 (truncated=2, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document heavily utilizes underlining to emphasize the topics of public comments in the bulleted lists on pages 3 and 4 (e.g., 'Environmental Justice', 'Risk Classification', 'Costs'). This underlining is completely lost in the JSON output, rendering these keywords as plain text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Underlined and bolded list item prefixes, e.g., "Environmental Justice –" and "Risk Classification –"
concerns.location: Pages 3 and 4, list items under sections II and III

action_items: Merge the split list items spanning pages 3 and 4 into a single continuous list structure.
Remove all retained header and footer text from the output.
Provide complete and descriptive alt text for the two images with truncated descriptions.
Review the lists on pages 3 and 4 and reapply underlining for emphasis if necessary for context.
